title: rhinanthus major subsp. serotinus
intro: Rhinanthus major subsp. serotinus, also known as late flowering yellow rattle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants, typically grasses. The plant is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Rhinanthus major subsp. serotinus can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and is said to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health.
